w:r.w:t: German Language Learning Platform + Agency ERP System

This document is the single source of truth for the new developer joining this project. The previous build has been discarded entirely. This is a full rebuild from zero. Read every section before writing a single line of code.

Phase 1 covers the complete Learning Platform + Agency ERP System. Phase 2 introduces Agentic AI features, adaptive learning, and cross-language expansion. All AI features are excluded from Phase 1 build.
Maximum 2 developers. You are the full-stack developer. One additional resource (UI/UX or part-time backend support) may be added. Architecture must be designed for this constraint.
Section 4 — Learning Platform: Full Feature Specification
Section 5 — Agency ERP System: Full Feature Specification
Section 8 — Phase 1 Build Scope & What is Excluded
ection 9 — Phase 2: Agentic AI Integration Strategy
We are building a unified digital platform that serves two distinct but deeply connected markets:
learning platform for students — combining live classes, recorded courses, CEFR-aligned content (A1 to C2), payments, and gamification.
A fully featured ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) system for German study agencies — enabling them to manage student o
nboarding, exam registration, accommodation, visa processing, and all related payments under one roof.
These two products are not separate applications. They share a single platform infrastructure, a unified payment layer, and a common user identity syste
m. A student who enrolls through an agency flows directly into the learning platform. An agency that manages 200 students has full visibility into every stage of each student's journey.
1.2 Why This Exists — The Market Problem
tly operate with a fragmented toolset. A typical agency uses:
A spreadsheet or basic CRM to track student enquiries and onboarding
Manual email chains to coordinate exam registrations (Goethe,
Separate invoicing tools for accommodation and v
Offline processes for document collection and KYC
No visibility into a student's learning progress after they leave the agency's direct sphere
This fragmentation costs agencies time, introduces errors, and makes scaling impossible. There is no si
ngle platform in the market that combines agency operations management with a live German learning environment.

We already have agency clients ready to buy this system. The ERP module is a direct revenue opportunity. These are B2B clients paying a platform subscription or per-student fee. This is not a future idea — it is a present commercial requirement.
Students pay to enroll in courses on the learning platform
Agencies pay a monthly or annual SaaS fee to use the ERP system
Agencies are billed per active student managed through ERP
Platform takes a service fee on exam bookings processed through ERP
Platform takes a commission on accommodation arranged via ERP
Platform deducts commission before releasing teacher earnings
The platform is structured as two logical products sharing a single codebase and infrastructure:
The two products are not siloed. The following connections exist at the data and workflow level:
A student onboarded by an agency through the ERP is automatically created as a user on the Learning Platform.
An agency can purchase course enrollments in bulk
for their students — payment flows through the ERP and enrollments are provisioned on the Learning Platform.
The agency can view their students' course progress, attendance, and certificate status directly from the ERP dashboard.
r courses, exams, accommodation, or visa processing — flow through the unified payment layer and are recorded against the student's profile.
Certificates issued on the Learning Platform are accessible from the ERP student profile and can be included in vis
a applications processed through the ERP.
The Platform Admin is the super-user who controls the entire system across both products.
Approves teacher registrations and KYC documents
Approves agency registrations and sets their subscription tier
Manages platform-wide commission and fee configuration
Views all revenue, user growth, and activity analytics across both products
Controls all role permissions and system configuration
Can block or unblock any user on the platfor
Teachers are verified instructors on the Learning Platform. They must complete KYC and receive Admin approval before accessing full functionality.
Create and publish Live, Recorded, and Hybrid courses
Upload recorded sessions and learning materials
Track student attendance, progress, and quiz results per course
View and respond to student feedback and ratings
Monitor earnings and submit payout requ
Students are the primary end-users of the Learning Platform. They may register independently or be onboarded by an agency through the ERP.
Register with email/phone or be created by an agency
Access recorded sessions and course materials
Track their own progress and CEFR level
Earn points, badges, and leaderboard rankings
View their own payment history for all transactions
The Agency Admin is the primary operator of the ERP system within a given agency. Each agency has one or
Onboard and manage students in bulk
Register students for German proficiency exams (Goethe,
Track and submit visa and documentation requirements
View student progress across all Learning Platform activities
Generate invoices and manage payment collection from students
nate accounts under an Agency Admin with limited operational access. Permissions are defined by the Agency Admin.
Process exam registrations on assigned cases
Section 4 — Learning Platform: Full Feature Specification

All features in this section are in-scope for Phase 1. The previous build attempted these features and produced a flawed, incomplete result. This is a clean rebuild — no code from the previous build should be referenced or reused.
Email and password registration with email verification
Phone number registration with OTP verification
Role-based account creation: Student, Teacher, Agency Admin, Agency Staff
Students created by agencies skip the public registration flow
Cookie consent and GDPR-compliant data handling
Multi-step onboarding wizard: personal bio, teaching experience, qualifications
Document upload: identity proof, qualification certificates
Language proficiency field and CEFR certification
Bank account and UPI payout details
n review queue with approve / reject controls
Pending state until approved — course creation locked until then
Teachers create courses of three delivery types: Live, Recorded, Hy
CEFR level tagging per course (A1, A2, B1, B2, C1, C2)
Custom pricing per course including free course option
Course preview module — teachers can designate modules as publicly viewable before enrollment
Cohort-based enrollment mode: fixed start date,
Drip content scheduling: modules released on a time-based schedule after enrollment
Course search and filter: by CEFR level, delivery type, price ceiling, keyword
Course rating and review system (1–5 stars + comment, one per
Student roster view for teachers: name, enrollment date, payment, completion %, progress bar
WebRTC peer-to-peer video and audio conferencing in-browser
ICE server configuration: Google STUN + Metered.ca TURN
UUID-based room ID tied to course and broadcast via Socket.IO event
Backend enforces enrollment before any student can enter a room
Teacher controls: mic toggle, camera toggle, End Session button
Student controls: mic toggle, camera toggle, Leave button
tomatic attendance tracking on session entry and exit
Live session start notification pushed to all enrolled students
[Phase 1] Interactive layer: real-time polls, student question queue, shared whiteboard
urse player: module sidebar with completion status + HTML5 video player
Students mark modules complete individually; progress saved to backend instantly
Progress bar updates without page reload
Downloadable study materials attached to modules
ent functionality on recorded session videos
Certificate issuance banner appears inline when final module is completed
Teachers create quizzes per course with multiple-choice and short-answer questions
hanism on quiz delivery (tab-switch detection, time limits)
Quiz answer review available to student after submission
Assignments created manually by teachers per module
Students submit assignments through their dashboard
Spaced repetition vocabulary flashcard system (built-in, not AI-dependent)
Automated CEFR placement assessment quiz at student registration
Enrolled courses list with progress indicators
reable learning portfolio (public URL + downloadable PDF): completed courses, certificates, CEFR level, quiz scores, streaks
Points awarded for: module completion (10 pts), live class attendance, quiz completion, streak maintenance
streak system: increments on consecutive active days, resets to 1 on missed day
Global leaderboard: top 20 non-blocked students ranked by cumulative points and streak
Skill badges for achievements (course completion, streak milestones, CEFR levels)
Discussion forum per course for enrolled students
Group chat rooms accessible to enrolled students
Moderation tools for teachers within their course community
ects with ERP agency if applicable)
UUID-based certificate ID for public verification
Public verification endpoint (no login required): returns student name, course, validity status
Certificate visible in student's shareable portfolio
Certificate synced to ERP student profile if student belongs to an
Dashboard polls notification API every 30 seconds
Live session notifications update via Socket.IO without page reload
Email automation for: registration, course e
nrollment, payment confirmation, teacher approval, certificate issuance, payout release
Support ticket system for student and teacher complaints
All tickets tracked through admin resolution workflow
Section 5 — Agency ERP System: Full Feature Specificatio

The Agency ERP is the primary B2B product. We have real agency clients who are ready to adopt this system. Build quality and UX here must be at a professional SaaS standard. This is what agencies are paying for.

The Agency ERP is a dedicated management console accessible by Agency Admins and Agency Staff. Each agency gets their own isolated workspace. Data is not shared across agencies. The Platform Admin can view all agencies.
Agency registers on the platform with business details, registration number, address, contact info
Platform Admin reviews and approves the agency
On approval, agency receives their dedicated ERP workspace
Admin configures the agency's subscription tier (Basic, Professional, Enterprise)
Agency Admin creates staff accounts within their workspace
es or bulk import via CSV
Student profile contains: personal details, contact info, passport info, language level, target exam, target intake date
Document repository per student: store passport scans, qualification certificates, visa documents, photos
Status pipeline: Enquiry → Application → Documentation → Enrolled → Exam Registered → Visa Applied → Departed
Filter and search students by status, in
take date, exam type, counsellor assigned
Activity log per student: every action, payment, status change is recorded with timestamp and staff name
Notes and follow-up reminders per student
ents for German proficiency exams: Goethe-
Track exam registration status per student: Pending → Registered → Fee Paid → Confirmed
Store and attach exam results and certificates to student profile
Automated reminders for upcoming exam dates
Bulk exam registration for cohorts of students
ings: student hostels, shared apartments, homestays
Each listing: property name, location, room types, availability, monthly cost, photos, rules
Book accommodation for a student: assign property, room type, move-in/out dates
Accommodation booking fee processed through the unified payment layer
Automated alerts for upcoming move-in dates and payment due dates
list builder per student: create a required document list based on visa type
Document upload by student (via their portal view) or by agency staff
Document status: Missing → Uploaded → Verified → Submitted to Embassy
Track visa application milestones: VFS appointment booked, application submitted, biometrics done, visa received
Store visa details: visa type, issue date, expiry date, entry conditions
Visa processing fee tracked and payable through the payment layer
Automated reminders for document deadlines and embassy appointment dates
Agency Admin can enroll their students in any Learning Platform course directly fr
Bulk enrollment: enroll a group of students into a course in one action
View each student's course progress, attendance, quiz scores, and certificate status from within the ERP
Certificates earned on the Learning Platform are automatically visib
le in the ERP student profile
Purchase course access in bulk at agency-negotiated pricing
Total active students, students by status, students by intake month
Revenue summary: total fees collected, outstanding balances, refunds
Exam registration pipeline: how many students registered, pending, confirmed per exam type
Accommodation occupancy rates and upcoming vacancies
Visa processing pipeline: how many applicat
Staff performance: cases handled per staff member
Students onboarded by an agency have a limited read-only view of their own ERP data. They can:
See their document checklist and upload documents
See their payment history for all transactions
This module tracks all money held, collected, and disbursed at the agency level — providing complete financial transparency and accountability for both the agency and the

The Agency Fund Management module provides a comprehensive ledger for all financial flows within an agency's operation. This is a dedicated financial control layer on top of the unified payment architecture.
Agency-level ledger: all inflows (student fee collections) and outflows (exam fees, accommodation, visa charges, platform subscription) recorded in a single chronological view
Per-student fund account: real-time outstanding balance,
total paid, and upcoming dues per student
Fund pool tracking: when agencies collect fees from multiple students for batch services (e.g., group exam registration), the system tracks the pooled amount against disbursements
h multi-currency fields for international agencies in Phase 2 expansion
Reconciliation view: compare agency-collected amounts vs platform-recorded amounts, with discrepancy flagging
Agency staff can log offline collections again
st a student account (cash, bank transfer) with receipt reference
Automated receipt generation for every recorded collection — downloadable PDF
Partial payment tracking: students paying in instalments are tracked with due date alerts
-coded aging report (current, 30 days overdue, 60+ days overdue)
Bulk statement generation: export all student balances for a given period
Exam fee disbursements: when exam registration fees are p
aid through the platform, the deduction is reflected against the student's fund account
Accommodation payment disbursements: tracked against bookings with payment proof attachments
Platform subscription fees: auto-deducted from agency balance on renewal wi
Refund management: when a student withdrawal or cancellation triggers a refund, the fund account is updated and the agency admin is notified
Agency payable reports: exportable monthly reports of all disbursements for accounting purposes
A tamper-resistant audit trail covering every significant action across the agency workspace — ensuring accountability, supporting compliance, and enabling dispute resolution.
Audit logs are automatically generated for all critical system events. They cannot be deleted or edited by any user, including Agency Admins. Only the Platform Admin can view cross-agency audit data.
Student record creation, updates, and de
letion attempts — with the changed fields captured in a before/after snapshot
Document uploads, verification actions, and document deletions — including who performed each action
Status pipeline transitions for every student — with timestamp, staff name, a
Exam registrations submitted, modified, or cancelled — with fee amounts and payment reference
Accommodation bookings created, modified, or cancelled — with associated payment records
Visa milestone updates — every status change with a
Payment collections logged by agency staff — amount, method, reference, and staff identity
Fund disbursements — with recipient, amount, payment method, and
Login and logout events for all agency accounts — including IP
Permission changes — when an Agency Admin modifies staff roles or access levels
Bulk operations — CSV imports, bulk status updates, bulk exam registrations
Filterable by: date range, staff member, action type, student affected, payment reference
Searchable by student name, reference ID, or keyword
Each log entry shows: timestamp (UTC), actor (user name + role), action type, affected entity, before/after snaps
Exportable as CSV or PDF for compliance reporting and external audits
Non-repudiation: all log entries are cryptographically signed server-side — no tampering possible at the application layer
Retention policy: audit logs retained for
a minimum of 7 years (configurable by Platform Admin per regulatory requirement)
Audit trail supports GDPR right-of-access requests — log entries tied to a specific student can be exported as part of a data subject request
Integration with the platform-level security audit log (Section 9.3) — agency actions feed into the master complia
Automated anomaly alerts: if a staff account performs an unusual volume of actions (bulk edits, mass deletions) in a short window, the Agency Admin and Platform Admin are notified
ents and can log these through the ERP
Exam fees, accommodation fees, and visa processing fees tracked per student
Bulk invoice generation for agency students
Agency subscription fee billed by the platform on a recu
Agency financial dashboard: total collected, outstanding, refunded

All money movements on the platform — course fees, exam fees, accommodation payments, visa processing fees, agency subscriptions — must pass through the platform's payment system. No off-platform payments are permitted. This is a hard requirement.
Primary payment gateway: Stripe (supports INR, cards, UPI, net banking)
All Stripe Checkout sessions created server-side
Payment records stored in DB at session creation time with platform commission and payee share pre-calculated
ion verification call confirms payment status and triggers downstream actions (enrollment, registration, booking)
Webhook handling for async payment events from Stripe
Exam body fee + Platform service fee
Complete payment history in student dashboard
Commission rate configured by Platform Admin (stored in Config collection, applied dynamically at checkout)
Platform commission and teacher share calculated and stored on every Payment record at purchase time
Per live class attendance earnings (where applicable)
Income report, tax statement, withdrawal system, payment history in teacher dashboard
Admin releases payout manually or in bulk — triggers notification to teacher
Agencies collect payments from students and can log these
Exam fees, accommodation fees, and visa processing fees tracked per student
Bulk invoice generation for agency students
Agency subscription fee billed by the platform on a recurring basis
ancial dashboard: total collected, outstanding, refunded
JWT access tokens (short expiry) + refresh tokens (long expiry, stored in
Role-based access control enforced on every backend endpoint via middleware
Agency tenancy enforced on every ERP endpoint via
ds prevent UI access to unauthorized sections
Admin can block any user — blocked users receive a frontend overlay preventing all access
All sensitive fields encrypted at rest where applica
File uploads validated for type and size before storage
No private keys or secrets committed to version control
GDPR-ready data structure: users can request data export and deletion
Privacy policy and terms of service pages
Audit log: all administrative actions recorded with timestamp and user (see Section 5.10 for full Agency Audit Log specification)
Quiz anti-cheat: tab-switch detection, time enforcement
r KYC and visa are stored securely and only accessible to
Full authentication system including Google OAuth
Agentic AI integration and analysis layer (Section 9)
AI Chatbot for language practice (Gemini integration)
Mobile app (architecture must be mobile-app-ready, but no native app in Phase 1)
Multi-language agency expansion (Section 10) — architecture prepared in Phase 1, activated in
Section 9 — Phase 2: Agentic AI Integration Strategy

All items in this section are excluded from Phase 1. However, the Phase 1 architecture must be designed with these integration points in mind. The developer is expected to assess current system capabilities, identify integration gaps, and produce a feasibi
lity and implementation report before Phase 2 begins.
Agentic AI refers to AI systems that do not merely respond to single prompts but autonomously plan, reason, take sequences of actions, and complete multi-step workflows with min
imal human intervention. Unlike a simple chatbot, an agentic system can:
the state of the platform and the data within it
Identify tasks that need to be done (e.g., students approaching exam dates without confirmed registrations)
Execute actions across the system (e.g., send reminders, flag issues, generate reports)
Operate continuously in the background or be triggered by sy
Use tools — database lookups, API calls, document parsing — to complete its tasks

Before any Phase 2 AI implementation begins, the developer must conduct a thorough analysis of the current platform state and produce a report. The implementation decision and timeline will be made based on that report.
every existing system module (Learning Platform, ERP, Payment Layer) and document its current data structures, API surface, and event hooks
Identify where agentic AI can add the most value — high-volume repetitive tasks, decision points that currently requ
ire human judgment, and data patterns that could be automated
Assess whether any existing AI/ML tools, third-party services, or LLM APIs are already partially integrated or tested
Document data readiness: which data is structured and
Identify integration constraints: latency, data privacy, API rate limits, cost per call
Produce a written report (the Agentic AI Feasibility and Implementation Report) covering: technical assessment, business case, risk analysis, p
roposed architecture, and phased rollout plan
The project team will review this report and make the go/no-go decision on Phase 2 implementation. No Phase 2 AI work begins without this report being accepted.
The following are the areas where agentic AI integr
ation is being considered. These are proposals — the developer's analysis will refine, add to, or remove items from this list.
Adaptive Learning Agent: analyses each student's quiz scores, attendance patterns, and progress ve
locity to recommend the next course or module, adjust difficulty, and generate a
Writing Evaluation Agent: assesses written German assignments against CEFR criteria, provides structured feedback, and flags submissions for human t
eacher review where confidence is low
Pronunciation Coach Agent: uses speech recognition to evaluate spoken German, identify phonetic errors, and deliver corrective feedback with example audio
Engagement Recovery Agent: monitors students who have not logge
d in for N days, identifies the likely drop-off cause (missed quiz, failed payment, scheduling conflict), and initiates a recovery sequence (
Chatbot Tutor Agent: provides conversational German prac
tice in a controlled dialogue environment — correcting grammar, explaining vocabulary in context, and adapting to the student's current CEFR level
Document Completeness Agent: reviews uploaded student documents against the required
checklist for their visa type, identifies missing or expiring documents, and automatically prompts the student and agency staff
Visa Timeline Agent: tracks visa application milestones against typical embassy processing times, flags students at risk of miss
ing intake dates, and recommends actions (expedited processing, alternative intake)
Exam Readiness Agent: cross-references student language level (from Learning Platform data) with target exam and date, and flags students who are unlikely to be ready — tri
Financial Anomaly Agent: monitors fund management ledgers for unusual patterns (unexpected disbursements, large unreconciled balances) and alerts the Agency Admin and Platform Admin
Student Lifecycle Agent: orchestrates the enti
re student journey from Enquiry to Departure, prompting the right staff at the right time, reducing manual tracking overhead
Revenue Intelligence Agent: analyses platform-wide revenue trends, identifies top-performing courses, ag
encies, and teachers, and generates weekly business intelligence summaries for the Platform Admin
Fraud Detection Agent: monitors payment events, login patterns, and document submissions for anomalies indicative of fraud or policy abuse
Support Triage Agent: analyses incoming support tickets,
by type and urgency, routes to the correct team,
and drafts suggested responses for common issues

This platform is not permanently tied to German language instruction. The architecture, ERP framework, and agentic AI layer are designed to support any language-promotion agency operating in any linguistic domain. This section defines how that expansion wo
The market for language promotion agencies extends well beyond German. Consider the analogous structures in operation globally:
Study-abroad and migration agencies across South Asia, Southeast Asia, Africa
Francophone immigration and study agencies globally
LATAM and Spanish immigration pathway agencies
Korean EPS-TOPIK migration agencies in Asia

All of these ecosystems share the same structural pattern: students learning a target language, agencies managing their pathway to certification, visa, or employment. The ERP model is directly transferable.
Phase 1 must be built with multi-language tenancy in mind, even though the first deployment is German-only. The following architectural decisions enable future expansion without a rebuild:
The course CEFR tagging system is
already framework-agnostic — while A1–C2 maps to German/French/Spanish, the level field can be overridden per language instance (e.g., N5–N1 for Japanese JLPT, Band 1–9 for IELTS)
Exam registration module stores exam body, exam name, and level as configura
ble fields — not hardcoded to Goethe/
Course content language is stored as a metadata attribute — a Japanese language course and a German language course are structurally identical in the database
array — the ERP interface adapts its exam registration screens based on these values
A Certification Body Registry is introduced as a platform-level configuration: each body has a name, supported languages, level schema, and typical registration fields
Agency Admins select from the Registry when setting up exam registrations — new bodies c
an be added by the Platform Admin without code changes
Result formats and certificate templates are configurable per body
The frontend is built with i18n (
) from the start — all UI strings stored in translatio
Phase 1 launches in English and German interface languages
Additional interface languages (French, Spanish, Japanese, etc.) added by dropping in a translation file — no frontend rebuild required
considered in the CSS architecture to enable Arabic or Urdu interface if required
Each language deployment can operate under its own subdomain or custom domain (e.g., french.platform.com or jpn-agency.com)
onboarding flow includes language scope selection — an agency specifies which language(s) they operate in
Platform Admin can create language-scoped admin views — a French-language platform admin sees only French agencies and courses
, and logo are configurable per language instance — supporting white-label partnerships with cultural institutes or government bodies
10.3 Expansion Pathway — What Changes Per Language
What Adapts for a New Language
New entries added to the Certification Body Registry by Platform Admin — no code change
Level mapping table updated (e.g., A1–C2 → N5–N1) — configurable in platform settings
to the target language and cultural context
Visa and documentation checklist templates updated per target country and language
New teacher cohort onboarded; content language-tagged in the existing content management system
New translation file added; no frontend code changes required
Language model switched or fine-tuned for target language; tool configurations remain identical
Same flow, with language scope field selecting the operating language context
Phase 1 does not require building multi-language features. But it requires building Phase 1 features in a way that does not foreclose expansion. Hardcoded language-specific values are a
) in business logic — store as configurable values
Never hardcode CEFR levels as fixed
in exam registration or course logic — treat them as configurable level schemas
e i18n libraries for all user-facing strings from day one
Store language scope in the Agency model and Course model as a
Design the Certification Body Registry as an extensible data structure, not a fixed list
Document all language-specific assumptions made during Phase 1 in a technical debt register — these become Phase 2 tasks
The team must not exceed 2 developers. Focus, ownership, and clean architect
You own the entire system end-to-end. Your responsibilities:
Overall system architecture and database design
Backend: all REST API endpoints, Socket.io server, authentication system, payment integration
Frontend: Next.js setup, routing, role-based dashboards, component architecture
: all CRUD, tenancy scoping, document management
Fund Management and Audit Log modules (Sections 5.9 and 5.10)
Agentic AI Feasibility and Implementation Report (Phase 2 prerequisite, Section 9.2)
Expansion readiness: ensure Phase 1 code does not foreclose
Code review of any work produced by Developer 2
Final sign-off on all delivered features before they are marked complete
second resource may be brought in for UI-heavy work. This person should:
Work only on frontend components: dashboards, forms, agency ERP UI, tables, modals
Follow the component and styling system established by Developer 1
Have no backend access or databa
Deliver pixel-accurate, responsive, accessible UI components
areas: Agency ERP student management views, Fund Management UI, Audit Log viewer, Admin dashboards, Teacher and Student dashboards
Weekly progress check-ins with the project manager
All features tracked against the specification in this document
No feature is marked complete until it passes functional testing end-to-end
Developer 1 maintains a running build log of what is done, what is in progress, and what is blocked
Any technical decision that deviates from this specification requires PM approval
Agentic AI Feasibility Report delivered before Phase 1 handoff — timing to
This document is the complete specification for Phase 1 and the roadmap for Phase 2.
If a feature is described here, it must be built. If it is not described here, it is not in scope.
Any questions must be raised with the project manager before implementation begins.
